title: What you need to know about Brazil vs. France at Gillette Stadium
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brazil and France will play a friendly match at Gillette Stadium on Thursday, with kickoff at 4 p.m.
The game will be viewable on ESPN2, ESPN Deportes, and streamed on ESPN+; ticket prices start at $230.
This match serves as a test run for World Cup preparations and offers player evaluation opportunities for both teams.

title: Closing arguments delivered in trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons. Here’s what happened.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Assistant Essex District Attorney argues Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, justifying his response.
Fitzsimmons's defense claims she was suicidal due to a restraining order and that Noonan acted too quickly.

title: 17-year-old killed in Brockton shooting, second fatal shooting in the city this week
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 17-year-old Jeffry Damestoire was fatally shot in Brockton; 18-year-old Tyree St. Cloud is charged with murder.
Incident marks the second fatal shooting in Brockton within a week, impacting local events and the community.
St. Cloud was arraigned on murder and firearm charges; he remains in custody awaiting further court proceedings.

title: Back Bay restaurant named among The Hollywood Reporter’s most anticipated global openings for 2026
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Rosa y Marigold, a new Back Bay restaurant, is listed among The Hollywood Reporter's most anticipated global openings for 2026.
It's the only New England restaurant featured, joining new venues in cities like Kyoto, Singapore, and New York.
The restaurant, created by Maria Rondeau and JuanMa Calderón, will offer Peruvian and Peruvian-Chinese dishes at 400 Newbury St.

title: Rep. Moulton prohibits staff from prediction market betting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton bans prediction market participation for all staff, citing ethics concerns and insider influence risks.
The policy applies to Moulton's congressional office staff and bars trading on political or geopolitical outcomes.
Moulton's stance emphasizes high ethical standards, urging other officials to reject profiting from government-related events.

title: Watch: Closing arguments in the Kelsey Fitzsimmons trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Closing arguments in Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial are being livestreamed via NBC10 Boston.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons, former North Andover police officer, testified saying she 'had just lost everything'.
The trial is being decided by a judge, not a jury, at Fitzsimmons's request.

title: Plymouth police officer, husband arrested amid allegations of sexual abuse
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth officer Samantha Pelrine and husband Daniel Forand were arrested on charges including aggravated child rape and indecent assault.
The alleged victim, under the couple's legal guardianship, reported sexual assaults began shortly after moving in with Pelrine and Forand.
The Plymouth Police Department condemned the allegations and has placed Officer Pelrine on administrative leave pending further investigation.

title: Jayson Tatum explains what’s allowed Jaylen Brown to turn into an MVP contender
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown's increased role has made him a contender for NBA MVP, leading the Celtics with career-high stats.
The Celtics defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-109, with Brown scoring 31 points and contributing eight rebounds and assists.
Brown attributes Boston's success to team development and leadership, along with Jayson Tatum's return from an Achilles injury.

title: In a fitting turn, Casey Mittelstadt delivered in one of Bruins’ gutsiest wins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Casey Mittelstadt scored his 14th goal to help the Bruins tie Buffalo 3-3 before winning in overtime.
The Boston Bruins secured a 4-3 road win over the Buffalo Sabres, putting them three points ahead in the playoff race.
Mittelstadt, initially a struggling asset, impresses in Marco Sturm's system, scoring 12 goals in 56 games this season.

title: Why is Jaylen Brown getting more trips to the free-throw line? ‘Maybe complaining’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown has increased his free-throw attempts amid an eventful season with NBA officials.
Complaints about officiating may have helped Brown receive more foul calls during games.
In a recent game, Brown made 12 of 14 free throws, contributing to Celtics' win over OKC Thunder.
